title: Sanamahi Lainingkol
tagline: Sanamahi Lainingkol — spiritual town of Manipur

overview.short: The Lainingthou Sanamahi Kiyong (Meitei for 'Lainingthou Sanamahi Shrine'), officially known as the Laiyingthou Sanamahi Kiyong (Meitei for 'Laiyingthou Sanamahi Shrine'), is a tem
overview.description: The Lainingthou Sanamahi Kiyong (Meitei for 'Lainingthou Sanamahi Shrine'), officially known as the Laiyingthou Sanamahi Kiyong (Meitei for 'Laiyingthou Sanamahi Shrine'), is a temple of God Lainingthou Sanamahi of Meitei religion (Sanamahism), built on the Nongmaiching mountain (Selloi Langmai mountain) in the Imphal East district of Kangleipak (Meitei for 'Manipur'). It is a center of the Sanamahism followers in Manipur. It is the central body of the "Sanamahi Lainingkol" (University of Sanamahi Culture) at Chingoi Maru Langmaiching (Nongmaiching). The Sanamahi Kiyong is a religious destinat

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Andro, Imphal East
topPlaces.description: Andro is a village and a nagar panchayat in Imphal East district in the state of Manipur, India. Andro is an earmarked Scheduled Caste village which has been developed to represent the cultural heritage and artful creativeness of the Manipuri tribes.

topPlaces.name: Keirao Bitra
topPlaces.description: Keirao Bitra is an administrative sub-division of Imphal East district in the northeastern state of Manipur, India. It is one of the three subdivisions of the district, alongside Porompat and Sawombung.

topPlaces.name: Iril River
topPlaces.description: The Iril River (Meitei: Iril Turel) is a river that runs through the eastern suburbs of the city of Imphal in the state of Manipur, India. Dolathabi Barrage Project is on the river at Leitanpokpi village.

topPlaces.name: Keikhu
topPlaces.description: Keikhu (also Keikhoo) is a village, itself consisting of two twin villages, in the Imphal East district of Manipur, India. The village in the south, known as Keikhu Kabui, is inhabited by the Kabui tribes, the early settlers of the place.

topPlaces.name: Porompat
topPlaces.description: Porompat (Meitei pronunciation: /pō-rōm-pāt/) is a census town in Imphal East district in the Indian state of Manipur.

topPlaces.name: Lamlai
topPlaces.description: Lamlai is a town, governed by a Municipal Council, in the Imphal East district of Manipur state of India.

topPlaces.name: Khongman
topPlaces.description: Khongman is a census town in Imphal East district in the Indian state of Manipur.

topPlaces.name: Khuman Lampak Main Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Khuman Lampak Main Stadium is a multi-use stadium located in Imphal, in the Indian state of Manipur. It is used mostly for football and athletics, and hosts the majority matches of the Manipur State League.

items.note: Andro is a village and a nagar panchayat in Imphal East district in the state of Manipur, India. Andro is an earmarked Scheduled Caste village which has been developed to represent the cultural heritage and artful creativeness of the Manipuri tribes.

Keirao Bitra is an administrative sub-division of Imphal East district in the northeastern state of Manipur, India. It is one of the three subdivisions of the district, alongside Porompat and Sawombung.

The Iril River (Meitei: Iril Turel) is a river that runs through the eastern suburbs of the city of Imphal in the state of Manipur, India. Dolathabi Barrage Project is on the river at Leitanpokpi village.

Keikhu (also Keikhoo) is a village, itself consisting of two twin villages, in the Imphal East district of Manipur, India. The village in the south, known as Keikhu Kabui, is inhabited by the Kabui tribes, the early settlers of the place.
Porompat (Meitei pronunciation: /pō-rōm-pāt/) is a census town in Imphal East district in the Indian state of Manipur.
Lamlai is a town, governed by a Municipal Council, in the Imphal East district of Manipur state of India.
Khongman is a census town in Imphal East district in the Indian state of Manipur.

The Khuman Lampak Main Stadium is a multi-use stadium located in Imphal, in the Indian state of Manipur. It is used mostly for football and athletics, and hosts the majority matches of the Manipur State League.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sanamahi Lainingkol?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sanamahi Lainingkol is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sanamahi Lainingkol?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Imphal Airport (15 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sanamahi Lainingkol?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sanamahi Lainingkol at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sanamahi Lainingkol?
faq.a: Stays in Sanamahi Lainingkol start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sanamahi Lainingkol?
faq.a: The top attractions are Andro, Imphal East, Keirao Bitra, Iril River, Keikhu, Porompat.
